target.name: Thinking Machines Lab
target.founder: Mira Murati, John Schulman, Barret Zoph, Lilian Weng

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $2.4B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Sakana AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Thinking Machines Lab is a Seed-stage AI & Software company founded by Mira Murati, John Schulman, Barret Zoph, and Lilian Weng, currently carrying a $12.0B post-money valuation on $2.0B raised. The peer universe comprises 9 comparable companies—1 public (Palantir) and 8 private entities including Sakana AI, Anthropic, and xAI—spanning Seed through Series A stages.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression uses the $12.0B last post-money as a base and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying $30.0B. Peer-Median anchors to a 4-company sample (Liquid AI at $2.4B, Field AI at $2.0B, Sakana AI at $1.0B, Twelve Labs at $250M), yielding a median of $1.5B with a range from $250M to $2.4B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in stage and capital raised within the AI sector.

Triangulation across both methods produces a range of $1.3B (low) to $34.5B (high), with a median of $15.8B. The extreme width—spanning more than 25x from floor to ceiling—reflects material tension between Stage-Progression (which leans on the company's stated $12.0B anchor) and Peer-Median (which suggests substantially lower valuations among comparable companies). Key uncertainties include whether the current $12.0B valuation reflects consensus market pricing or represents an outlier, and the relevance of public comparables given the nascent stage of monetization across frontier AI ventures.
